title: DNA extraction and precipitation
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"e0bt4","text":"Briefly, the DNA solution is extracted with phenol / chloroform / isoamyl alcohol in a ratio of 25/24/1 to remove protein contaminants and then precipitated with 100% ethanol or isopropanol. It is then washed with 70% ethanol to remove remaining organic molecules and the pellet is resuspended in a volume of interest.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Add 1 volume of 25/24/1 phenol / chloroform / isoamyl alcohol.
Mix by vortexing for 00:00:10 13000 rpm, °C, 15 seconds or more at Room temperature .
Take the aqueous phase (the upper one) using a 200 µL pipette; transfer to new tube.

Add 1/10 of 3M sodium acetate, pH 5.2 . And mix by vortexing or tapping. An alternative way is to extract using chloroform / isoamyl alcohol, this serves to prevent phenol residues, which is not important if you wash well with 70% ethanol.
Add 2 or 2.5 vol of °C 100% Ethanol. Mix well and leave at -20 °C freezer. (minimum 00:30:00 , one hour preferrable)
13000 rpm, °C, 5 minutes and discard the supernatant.
Add 70% ethanol (RNase Free) 1 mL at Room temperature , invert several times and microcentrifuge as in step 6.
Discard the supernatant and dry for 00:05:00 at 37 °C (repeat)
creator: Marco Cacciabue
